content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Your Angular app is running change detection 300 times per second right now.
No error. No warning. No red text in the console.
Just a dashboard that feels heavy, a scroll that stutters, and a client who says “it feels slow” without being able to explain why.
I spent two weeks hunting this exact problem in a fintech dashboard. I blamed the charts. I blamed the API. I blamed the browser. Then I opened the Angular DevTools profiler on a Friday evening and found the truth in ninety seconds.
One mistake. One pattern I had copied into a dozen components without ever questioning it.
And here is the scary part. I guarantee this mistake is sitting in your codebase right now, because it is invisible, it works perfectly, and nobody ever reviews it.
Let me show you where it hides.
The Mistake: You Are Running High-Frequency Events Inside Angular’s Zone
Every Angular app ships with Zone.js. Its job sounds harmless: watch every async event in the browser and tell Angular “something happened, go check the components.”
Read that again. Every async event. Every single one.
